tasks.title: Browser adapter planning
tasks.ask: Design a browser-connected companion surface that can inspect pages, save routines, and return readable results safely.

tasks.title: Support resolution planning
tasks.ask: Plan a safe autonomous customer-support workflow that resolves simple refund and escalation cases while preserving trust.

tasks.title: Incident triage handoff
tasks.ask: Triage a production incident, propose the safest first response, and return a reviewable operator handoff packet.

tasks.title: Experimental adapter review
tasks.ask: Review a proposed experimental adapter and return the safest first implementation cut with explicit review posture.

metrics.label: Delegation clarity
metrics.description: How clearly the task divides into actionable roles or steps.

metrics.label: Clarification pressure
metrics.description: How many follow-up questions are still obviously required before execution.

metrics.label: Artifact specificity
metrics.description: How specific and reviewable the expected outputs are.

metrics.label: Operator confidence
metrics.description: How inspectable the task shape is before execution widens.

metrics.label: Adoption overhead
metrics.description: How much extra implementation burden the lane introduces for an external team.

runs.title: Browser adapter planning
runs.ask: Design a browser-connected companion surface that can inspect pages, save routines, and return readable results safely.

reference_shape.title: Multi-agent coding formula
reference_shape.objective: coordinate an AI-first development team that writes, reviews, tests, and prepares deployment safely

metrics.note: Higher is better. This reflects how quickly participants can divide the work without renegotiating intent.
Lower is better. This estimates how many obvious follow-up questions still remain before safe execution.
Higher is better. This reflects how concrete and reviewable the expected outputs are.
Higher is better. This reflects how inspectable the work is before execution widens.
Lower is better. This reflects how much extra implementation burden the lane introduces for an outside team.

notes: Baseline carrier with no extra shaping layer.
Task, messages, and artifacts travel, but much of the structure is negotiated later.

metrics.note: Higher is better. This reflects how quickly participants can divide the work without renegotiating intent.
Lower is better. This estimates how many obvious follow-up questions still remain before safe execution.
Higher is better. This reflects how concrete and reviewable the expected outputs are.
Higher is better. This reflects how inspectable the work is before execution widens.
Lower is better. This reflects how much extra implementation burden the lane introduces for an outside team.

notes: A2A remains the carrier, but SAE adds a neutral shared work-shape.
This lane is the intended public adoption target.

metrics.note: Higher is better. This reflects how quickly participants can divide the work without renegotiating intent.

notes: A richer internal compiler shapes the work first, then exports into SAE for outside exchange.
This lane is richer, but also heaviest for outside adopters.

runs.summary: This draft POC run was generated locally from the SAE benchmark harness and an internal task compiler demo. It is reproducible, but it is not yet independently graded by an outside evaluator.
